questions.question: Why is the 'Golden Rule' (Treat others as you want to be treated) considered a failure in sales?

options.text: Because prospects are often dishonest about what they want
Because treating a prospect like yourself fails if they have a different personality type
Because it is too polite for modern aggressive sales
Because it implies you should give away product for free

questions.explanation: If you are a detailed analyst (C-Type) and you treat a fast-paced driver (D-Type) with long explanations, you will lose the deal. You must use the Platinum Rule: Treat them how THEY want to be treated.

questions.question: Which two axes define the DISC model?

options.text: Introversion vs. Extroversion and Thinking vs. Feeling
Pace (Fast vs. Measured) and Focus (Task vs. People)
Optimism vs. Pessimism and Logic vs. Emotion
Strategic vs. Tactical and Internal vs. External

questions.explanation: DISC maps behavior on a Motor Axis (Pace) and a Compass Axis (Focus).

questions.question: A prospect cuts you off 2 minutes into the call and says 'Just show me the pricing and the bottom line.' Which quadrant is this behavior signaling?

options.text: Dominant (D)
Influential (I)
Steady (S)
Conscientious (C)

questions.explanation: Dominant types are Fast-Paced and Task-Oriented. They value efficiency and results over politeness.

questions.question: Why do technical founders (often C-types) struggle to sell to Influential (I-type) buyers?

options.text: Technical founders are too aggressive
I-types are not smart enough to understand the tech
Technical founders over-explain details while I-types want vision and relationship
I-types only buy from large corporations

questions.explanation: I-types are Bored by details. They buy vision, excitement, and relationship. The technical founder's natural tendency to explain 'how it works' kills the I-type's enthusiasm.

questions.question: You represent a startup selling a new, innovative tool. Which personality type will be the HARDEST to close because they fear change and risk?

options.text: Dominant (D)
Influential (I)
Steady (S)
Conscientious (C)

questions.explanation: Steady (S) types value stability, security, and proven methods. They are the most resistant to change and 'risky' startups.
